Gather all required documents in self-attested photocopies along with originals.
Visit Building No. 30-D, Space Applications Centre, ISRO, Jodhpur Tekra, Ahmedabad – 380015, on any working day between 20 June and 3 July 2026 during 10:00–13:00 hrs.
Enter your name in the attendance sheet on arrival - this is mandatory to confirm your order in the first-come-first-serve queue.
Submit your application personally at the Recruitment Section.
Applications submitted by post or email will not be considered under any circumstances.

content_sections.heading: SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Quick Overview
content_sections.text: SAC ISRO is recruiting Graduate, Technician & Trade Apprentice Posts with Degree, Diploma, or ITI as qualification, Walkin on 20-06-2026 to 03-07-2026. Download SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Official Notification PDF

rows.information: Department of Space, Government of India
Building No. 30-D, SAC-ISRO, Jodhpur Tekra, Ahmedabad – 380015

content_sections.heading: What Is the SAC ISRO Apprenticeship Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: SAC ISRO's apprenticeship program is a structured, government-regulated training initiative conducted under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The Space Applications Centre at Ahmedabad - responsible for remote sensing, satellite communication, and space-based applications research - offers hands-on training exposure to fresh graduates, diploma holders, and ITI-qualified candidates in technical and non-technical streams. This is not a permanent job offer. The apprenticeship is a one-year training contract that gives candidates valuable on-the-job experience within ISRO's operational environment - a significant credential for any engineering or science fresher.

content_sections.heading: SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Post Codes and Disciplines
content_sections.text: SAC has notified 16 post codes covering three categories of apprentices. Each category has distinct educational requirements and eligible disciplines. Graduate Apprentice (Post Codes 01–05) Graduate Apprentices must hold a B.E. / B.Tech. / B.Arch. / B.Com / B.Lib or an equivalent qualification from a Government-recognized University, Board, or Institute with a minimum aggregate of 60% marks. Technician Apprentice (Post Codes 06–08) Technician Apprentices must have a Diploma in Engineering or Technology in the relevant field with First Class from a recognized State Board of Technical Education. Trade Apprentice (Post Codes 09–16) Trade Apprentices must have passed SSC / Matriculation / 10th and hold an ITI (NTC/STC) certificate in the relevant trade from a recognized institute.

content_sections.heading: SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates should carefully read all eligibility conditions before appearing for the walk-in. Appearing without meeting these conditions will result in disqualification. Nationality Only Indian Nationals are eligible to apply for SAC ISRO Apprentice 2026. State-Wise Eligibility Condition This is one of the most important eligibility filters and is frequently misunderstood by applicants. Graduate and Technician Apprentices (Post Codes 01–08): Candidates must have obtained their Degree or Diploma from a Government-recognized University/Board/Institute located in any of the following states/UTs: Maharashtra Gujarat Goa Chhattisgarh Madhya Pradesh Rajasthan Dadra Nagar Haveli & Daman & Diu (UT) Trade Apprentices (Post Codes 09–16): Candidates must have completed their ITI (NTC/STC) from a recognized institute in Gujarat State only. Educational Qualification Graduate Apprentice: Minimum 60% aggregate in B.E./B.Tech./B.Arch./B.Com/B.Lib or equivalent. Technician Apprentice: First Class in Diploma in Engineering/Technology in the relevant discipline. Trade Apprentice: SSC/10th pass + ITI (NTC/STC) in the relevant trade. Age Limit Age is calculated as on the date of joining. Age Relaxation: OBC (Non-Creamy Layer): 3 years relaxation in upper age SC / ST candidates: 5 years relaxation in upper age PwBD candidates: As per Government of India rules Year of Passing Condition Candidates who passed their Degree/Diploma/ITI in or after July 2023 are eligible. Candidates who have completed three or more years since obtaining their qualifying degree as on the expected date of joining are not eligible, even if they passed after July 2023. Prior Apprenticeship or Work Experience Candidates who have already completed or are currently undergoing any apprenticeship training, or who have one year or more of job experience as on the date of joining, are not eligible.

content_sections.heading: SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Stipend
content_sections.text: The monthly stipend offered to selected apprentices is fixed by the Government and varies by apprentice category. The training period is 12 months from the date of execution of the Apprenticeship contract.

content_sections.heading: SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Walk-In Schedule and Venue
content_sections.text: Unlike most central government recruitment processes, this apprenticeship follows a walk-in, first-come-first-serve model. No online application or postal submission is accepted. Walk-In Dates : 20 June 2026 to 03 July 2026 Days : All working days (Saturdays, Sundays, and declared holidays excluded) Walk-In Timing : 10:00 hrs to 13:00 hrs Venue : Building No. 30-D (Recruitment Section), Space Applications Centre, ISRO, Jodhpur Tekra, Ahmedabad – 380015 Important: On arrival at SAC, candidates must enter their details in the attendance sheet to confirm their order of appearance. This is mandatory.

content_sections.heading: SAC ISRO Apprentice Recruitment 2026 – Documents Required
content_sections.text: Candidates must carry self-attested photocopies of all required documents along with originals at the time of the walk-in. Missing documents may lead to rejection of the application on the spot. Mandatory documents for all candidates: Proof of Date of Birth (10th passing certificate / School Leaving Certificate / Birth Certificate) Aadhaar Card and PAN Card SSC (10th) Marksheet and Certificate Qualifying Exam Marksheets & Certificates: Trade Apprentices: ITI (NTC/STC) in the relevant trade Technician Apprentices: Final/Provisional Diploma + all semester marksheets Graduate Apprentices: Final/Provisional Degree + all semester marksheets Latest OBC (NCL – FY 2026-27) Certificate, if applicable Caste/Tribe Certificate (SC/ST), if applicable PwBD Certificate, if applicable EWS Income and Asset Certificate, if applicable Local Police Verification cum Character Certificate (in Original) Blood Group Report SBI Bank Account details Portal Registration (mandatory before walk-in): Trade Apprentices: Register at https://apprenticeshipindia.org Graduate & Technician Apprentices: Register at https://nats.education.gov.in

content_sections.heading: How to Apply
content_sections.text: The application process is entirely offline and in-person. There is no provision for online, email, or postal submission of applications. Download and fill the official application form attached to the SAC notification (available on the official ISRO/SAC website). Register on the appropriate apprenticeship portal (NATS or Apprenticeship India) before appearing for the walk-in. Gather all required documents in self-attested photocopies along with originals. Visit Building No. 30-D, Space Applications Centre, ISRO, Jodhpur Tekra, Ahmedabad – 380015, on any working day between 20 June and 3 July 2026 during 10:00–13:00 hrs. Enter your name in the attendance sheet on arrival - this is mandatory to confirm your order in the first-come-first-serve queue. Submit your application personally at the Recruitment Section. Applications submitted by post or email will not be considered under any circumstances.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: There is no written examination or interview for this apprenticeship. Selection is done purely on a first-come-first-serve basis, subject to verification of documents and eligibility. Candidates appearing earlier in the walk-in period have a higher chance of selection, given limited seats. If any discrepancy is found in name, age, qualification results, year of passing, or caste/tribe details at any stage, the candidature will be immediately disqualified. Candidates who do not meet medical fitness criteria at the time of joining will have their candidature cancelled automatically.

content_sections.heading: Important Terms and Conditions
content_sections.text: Understanding these conditions is critical before appearing for the walk-in, as violations at any stage lead to disqualification. Apprentices will have no claim for regular employment at SAC-ISRO, any other ISRO Centre, or under the Department of Space after completion of training. The apprenticeship contract is registered under the Apprentices Act, 1961 and Amendment Act, 1973. Candidates in existing Government service must disclose this in their application. Seats are limited; early walk-in increases your chance of selection. All decisions regarding eligibility, selection, and joining rest with SAC-ISRO administration.

content_sections.heading: Why Apply for SAC ISRO Apprenticeship 2026?
content_sections.text: For engineering and science freshers, an apprenticeship at ISRO's Space Applications Centre is a rare opportunity that carries significant weight in the job market and for higher studies abroad. Here's why candidates across disciplines actively seek this opportunity: Prestigious credential - ISRO is among the world's leading space agencies; SAC-specific experience adds unique value to a resume. Government-regulated stipend - Monthly stipend of up to ₹12,300 during the training year. Hands-on technical exposure - Direct involvement in space-application projects across electronics, mechanical, civil, and IT domains. NATS/Apprenticeship India certification - Completion leads to a nationally recognized apprenticeship certificate useful for both private-sector jobs and government competitive exams. No bond or service obligation - Unlike some training programs, there is no post-training bond. Source: Official Notice No. SAC: RMT: APP: 2026 dated 08.06.2026, issued by the Administrative Officer, Space Applications Centre (ISRO), Ahmedabad.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for SAC ISRO Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: The walk-in registration closes on 3 July 2026. Candidates must appear in person at Building No. 30-D, SAC-ISRO, Ahmedabad between 20 June and 3 July 2026 (working days only) during 10:00–13:00 hrs. There is no online application; hence no "last date" in the traditional sense - first-come-first-serve applies.

faq.question: Q2. What is the stipend for SAC ISRO Graduate Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: The monthly stipend for a Graduate Apprentice at SAC ISRO is ₹12,300 per month. Technician Apprentices receive ₹10,900 per month, and Trade Apprentices receive ₹10,560 or ₹11,040 per month depending on the post code.

faq.question: Q3. Is there a written exam for SAC ISRO Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: No. There is no written examination or interview. Selection is done on a first-come-first-serve basis based on document verification at the walk-in venue.

faq.question: Q4. Who is eligible for SAC ISRO Technician Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates holding a Diploma in Engineering or Technology in Electronics & Communication, Mechanical, or Civil Engineering with First Class from a recognized State Board of Technical Education are eligible. The Diploma must be from an institute in Maharashtra, Gujarat, Goa, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, or Dadra Nagar Haveli & Daman & Diu, and must have been obtained on or after July 2023.

faq.question: Q5. Can candidates from Tamil Nadu or other states apply for SAC ISRO Apprentice 2026?
faq.answer: For Graduate and Technician Apprenticeships (Post Codes 01–08), only candidates who obtained their Degree/Diploma from institutes in Maharashtra, Gujarat, Goa, Chhattisgarh, Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, or Dadra Nagar Haveli & Daman & Diu are eligible. Candidates from Tamil Nadu or other states are not eligible for these posts. For Trade Apprenticeships (Post Codes 09–16), ITI qualification must be from Gujarat State only.

faq.question: Q6. Will I get a permanent job at ISRO after completing this apprenticeship?
faq.answer: No. As per the official terms and conditions, apprentices will have no claim for employment at SAC-ISRO or any other ISRO Centre/Unit after completion of training. The program is purely for training purposes.

faq.question: Q7. What documents are needed for the SAC ISRO Apprentice walk-in?
faq.answer: You need: Aadhaar Card, PAN Card, Date of Birth proof, 10th marksheet and certificate, all qualifying exam marksheets (Degree/Diploma/ITI), caste/category certificate (if applicable), Local Police Verification & Character Certificate (original), Blood Group Report, and SBI Bank Account details. Additionally, registration on NATS or Apprenticeship India portal is mandatory.

faq.question: Q8. What is the age limit for SAC ISRO Trade Apprentices?
faq.answer: For Trade Apprentices (Post Codes 09–16), the age limit is 18 to 35 years as on the date of joining. OBC (NCL) candidates get 3 years of relaxation, and SC/ST candidates get 5 years of relaxation in the upper age limit.

faq.question: Q9. How many years after passing ITI/Diploma/Degree can I apply?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed their qualifying exam (Degree/Diploma/ITI) on or after July 2023, and must not have completed 3 years since passing as on the expected date of joining. Even if you passed after July 2023, you are ineligible if 3 years have elapsed by the time of joining.

faq.question: Q10. What is the contact number for SAC ISRO Apprentice 2026 inquiry?
faq.answer: For general inquiries related to SAC ISRO Apprentice 2026, call 079-26913021 during 10:00–12:30 hrs and 14:00–17:30 hrs, Monday to Friday.
